blocks.body: The science of collecting, organizing, analyzing, and interpreting data.
Information, often numerical, collected through observation or measurement.

A population is the entire group being studied. A sample is a subset of the population, used when studying the whole population is impractical. A random sample gives each member an equal chance of being chosen.
Data that falls into named groups or categories. Example: favorite color, type of pet.
Data that is measured or counted. Discrete: countable (number of siblings). Continuous: any value in a range (height, weight).
A biased sample does not represent the population well. Example: surveying only students in the math club about math opinions would over-represent math enthusiasts.
